title: theobroma cacao subsp. sativum
intro: Theobroma cacao subsp. sativum, also known as the cultivated cacao plant, is a subspecies of the Theobroma cacao tree, which is native to the Americas and is the primary source of cacao beans. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Theobroma cacao subsp. sativum include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of certain diseases, such as type 2 diabetes. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and chocolates, which can be made by infusing the cacao beans in hot water or extracting the active compounds using a solvent.
